title: Timer scheduler code must use port-valid timer ids and matching scheduler APIs
symptoms: Device raises ValueError: invalid Timer number on ports that do not support virtual timers
main.py calls Scheduler(timer_id=-1) on a non-RP2/non-Zephyr board
main.py calls sc.register(...) but timer_sched.py only defines add_task(...)
Fixing Timer(-1) is followed by AttributeError for a missing Scheduler.register method
wrong_pattern: Using virtual Timer(-1) from main.py on ports other than RP2/Pico and Zephyr, relying on Scheduler() when the scaffold scheduler default is timer_id=-1, or composing main.py against a scheduler API that is not implemented by the scaffold scheduler module.
correct_pattern: Keep scaffold-owned firmware/lib/scheduler/timer_sched.py unchanged unless the user explicitly approves a library contract change. The library default timer_id=-1 is valid for RP2/Pico and Zephyr virtual timers. For other MCU/port targets, fix the entrypoint assembly instead: instantiate Scheduler with an explicit valid non-negative hardware timer id, for example Scheduler(timer_id=0, error_cb=..., tick_ms=...). Ensure main.py task registration calls match the actual scheduler module API, for example add_task(callback, interval_ms, name=...).
root_cause_analysis: This is not just an LLM model hallucination. The skill allowed two independently generated surfaces, main assembly and scheduler implementation, to drift without a contract.
The generation workflow lacked a port-specific hardware API evidence gate for machine.Timer and confused library defaults with target-specific entrypoint assembly.
The validation workflow lacked an API conformance check between main.py and firmware/lib/scheduler/timer_sched.py.
PC tests and static checks can pass when they do not instantiate the exact MicroPython scheduler path on the target port.
fix_guidance: Read the official machine and ESP32 timer docs before generating timer scheduler code.
If target port is not RP2/Pico/RP2040/RP2350 or Zephyr, do not generate Timer(-1) or Scheduler(timer_id=-1) in main.py.
If main.py calls Scheduler(...), inspect Scheduler.__init__ defaults; Scheduler() is invalid on hardware-timer ports when the default timer_id maps to Timer(-1).
Do not rewrite scaffold scheduler internals to ticks_ms as a default fix; that breaks the RP2/Pico software-timer contract.
For hardware Timer scheduler mode, pass an explicit valid non-negative timer id from main.py and document why it is safe for the selected MCU.
Make main.py and timer_sched.py share one API contract. Do not call register unless timer_sched.py defines register.
Add a generate quality gate that imports/parses main.py and scheduler module to verify task registration methods exist.
If rotating logger is installed, keep scaffold logger source unchanged and mix timestamp/uptime into generated logger message text at call sites.
